mission.title: Install and Configure kgateway on Kubernetes
mission.description: kgateway is a resilient, performance-oriented control plane that implements the Kubernetes Gateway API for Envoy. It is the most widely deployed gateway in Kubernetes for microservices, supporting API gateway, hybrid application migration, and complex tenancy environments.

steps.title: Install Gateway API CRDs
steps.description:
Install the Kubernetes Gateway API CRDs which kgateway requires.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api/releases/download/v1.2.1/standard-install.yaml
```

steps.title: Install kgateway CRDs via Helm
steps.description:
Install the kgateway CRDs from the repository's Helm chart.
```bash
git clone https://github.com/kgateway-dev/kgateway.git
cd kgateway
helm install kgateway-crds install/helm/kgateway-crds --namespace kgateway-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Install kgateway using Helm
steps.description:
Deploy kgateway using the Helm chart from the repository.
```bash
helm install kgateway install/helm/kgateway --namespace kgateway-system
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that kgateway pods are running and the Gateway API resources are available.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kgateway-system
kubectl get gatewayclass
```

steps.title: Create a sample Gateway
steps.description:
Create a Gateway resource to verify the installation is working.
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: example-gateway
  namespace: kgateway-system
spec:
  gatewayClassName: kgateway
  listeners:
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    protocol: HTTP
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show kgateway pods running in the kgateway-system namespace with the GatewayClass registered. You can then create Gateway and HTTPRoute resources to manage traffic routing via Envoy.

uninstall.title: Remove Gateway resources
uninstall.description: Delete all Gateway and HTTPRoute resources before uninstalling kgateway.

uninstall.title: Uninstall kgateway Helm releases
uninstall.description: Remove the kgateway and kgateway-crds Helm releases.

uninstall.title: Clean up namespace
uninstall.description: Delete the kgateway-system namespace and remove Gateway API CRDs if no longer needed.

upgrade.title: Pull latest source
upgrade.description: Update the kgateway repository to get the latest Helm charts.

upgrade.title: Upgrade via Helm
upgrade.description: Run helm upgrade for both the CRDs and the main kgateway chart.

troubleshooting.title: GatewayClass not found
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure the kgateway CRDs were installed before the main chart.
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep gateway
```

troubleshooting.title: Gateway stuck in pending
troubleshooting.description:
Check the kgateway controller logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway --namespace kgateway-system
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm, kubectl, and git installed.
